2606.13115.title: G-Long: Graph-Enhanced Memory Management for Efficient Long-Term Dialogue Agents
2606.13115.paper_summary: While Large Language Models (LLMs) have advanced open-domain dialogue systems, maintaining long-term consistency remains a challenge due to inherent limitations in long-context reasoning and the inefficiency of processing extensive raw text. Existing approaches typically rely on either unstructured memory storage, which is prone to information loss, or computationally expensive LLMs that incur high latency. To address these limitations, we propose G-Long, a graph-enhanced framework that utilizes a fine-tuned small Language Model (sLM) for structured triplet extraction and associative retrieval, significantly reducing operational costs. Furthermore, we introduce the novel attention-aware importance scoring mechanism that leverages the intrinsic cross-attention signals of a T5 summarizer to identify salient memories. Extensive experiments across diverse benchmarks demonstrate that G-Long achieves state-of-the-art performance in both response generation and memory retrieval, yielding performance gains of up to 9.8% in response quality on MSC and 40.8% in retrieval recall on LME, while significantly minimizing computational overhead.
2606.13115.paper_summary_zh: 雖然大型語言模型（LLMs）在開放領域對話系統方面取得了進展，但由於長期一致性維護的固有限制，長期一致性仍然是一個挑戰，這些限制包括長上下文推理的固有限制和處理大量原始文本的低效率。現有的方法通常依賴於非結構化的記憶存儲，這容易導致信息丟失，或計算成本高昂的LLMs，這會產生高延遲。為了解決這些限制，我們提出了G-Long，一個圖增強框架，利用微調的小型語言模型（sLM）進行結構化三元組提取和關聯檢索，顯著降低操作成本。此外，我們引入了一種新穎的注意力感知重要性評分機制，利用T5摘要模型的內在交叉注意信號來識別顯著的記憶。廣泛的實驗在多個基準上表明，G-Long在響應生成和記憶檢索方面達到了最先進的性能，在MSC上的響應質量提高了高達9.8%，在LME上的檢索召回率提高了40.8%，同時顯著降低了計算開銷。
2606.13115.author: Minjun Choi et.al.
2606.13115.authors: Minjun Choi, Yoonjin Jang, Sangwon Youn, Youngjoong Ko
